AI demos are cheap. Trust is expensive.

In finance, one confident wrong answer can burn more than your entire software budget.

Leni is going after the part that actually matters: verified outputs, serious reasoning, and investment work you don’t have to babysit.

This is AI

Your brand guide is useless if your AI can’t follow it in motion.

Text, decks, and landing pages were the easy part.

Video is where most agents start improvising, breaking style, and turning “brand consistency” into chaos.

HeyGen’s frame.md is a big step toward AI agents that

Claude Code can now spawn hundreds of AI agents 
from a single prompt.

It writes its own orchestration script, breaks your 
task into subtasks, and runs them all in parallel.

One developer used it to port 750,000 lines of code 
in 11 days.

Here's how dynamic workflows actually

Traditional Claude Code handles tasks one at a time. 
You type a request, it works through it sequentially.

Dynamic workflows are different.

Claude reads your prompt, writes a JavaScript 
orchestration script on the fly, then spins up 
10 to 1,000 sub-agents that each tackle a

Here's the exact prompt structure I use to trigger 
a dynamic workflow in Claude Code:

"Create a workflow to [task]. Break it into parallel 
subtasks. For each unit of work: 1) complete the task, 
2) run an adversarial review to find issues, 3) apply 
fixes. Success criteria:

The numbers from early access:

Jarred Sumner (creator of Bun) ported his entire 
JavaScript runtime from Zig to Rust.

750,000 lines of generated Rust.
99.8% of the existing test suite passing.
11 days from first commit to merge.

Hundreds of agents ran in parallel, with two

I tested every major Claude feature the week it 
shipped. Dynamic workflows included.

My full Claude Skills Pack breaks down exactly how 
to structure prompts for workflows, effort controls, 
and every other feature most people don't know exists.

https://t.co/4GX8faM0Ph
